Nora retires after a fulfilling Monday; she had good social time, exercise, creative pursuit, and solid work output—full night's sleep ends by 6am next day.

diary: Woke up early enough to ease into the day—showered, had coffee at home in Ballard. The bike ride to First Hill was perfect, that early-June air still cool. Meetings ran long this morning, but I needed the caffeine anyway, so I ducked out to Vivace around ten. Then the whole office basically emptied when the World Cup match started. I ended up at Kremwerk with half the team, watching USA take on Wales. The place was packed, everyone yelling at the screen. Grabbed lunch there too—seemed silly to leave. Biked back to the office feeling a little guilty about the long break, but also energized. Afternoon was solid: design reviews, a few stakeholder calls. By three, I needed to move. Headed out to Gas Works Park with my camera instead of going straight home. The light was golden and perfect, caught some good shots of the city from up there. Rode home through Wallingford as the sun started dropping. Made dinner, spent the evening editing film photos on my laptop. Felt good—a day that mixed work, spontaneity, and something creative.
thought_process: The maintenance closure between Westlake and University District didn't affect me since I bike everywhere anyway, but it's funny how you notice infrastructure work when you're literally in the business of maintaining it. The World Cup match was a spur-of-the-moment thing—someone mentioned it, and I realized I hadn't taken a real break in weeks. I almost felt like I should stay at my desk, but that's the kind of thinking that burns you out. The afternoon work felt more focused after getting out and socializing. Gas Works Park was deliberate: I've been meaning to shoot more film, and the weather was too good to waste. Photography helps me think differently than engineering does. By evening, I was tired but satisfied—the kind of tired that comes from actually using your whole self.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
